findings.message: Irreversible destructive action with no confirmation: DeleteButton fires a DELETE request immediately on click. A misclick permanently removes the item with no undo or confirmation prompt.
findings.suggestion: Show a confirmation step before deleting (e.g., a confirm dialog or a two-step 'Delete' → 'Confirm' affordance), or provide an undo toast after deletion.

findings.message: No feedback on a mutating action: the Delete button is not disabled while the DELETE request is in flight and shows no pending/success/error indication. Users may double-click or assume nothing happened on slow networks.
findings.suggestion: Track a pending state, disable the button (and/or show a spinner) while the request is in flight, and surface success/error feedback rather than relying on window.location.reload().

findings.message: Error path is silently swallowed: the awaited fetch has no .catch / try/catch, and the unconditional window.location.reload() hides failures. A failed DELETE looks identical to a successful one, leaving users confused about whether the item was actually removed.
findings.suggestion: Check response.ok, surface an inline error message on failure, and only refresh the list (preferably via state update, not a full page reload) on success.

findings.message: Full-page reload after delete is a jarring flow: it discards scroll position, focus, and any in-progress UI state instead of updating the list in place.
findings.suggestion: Update local state (e.g., lift items into a parent or use a callback/mutation) to remove the deleted item without reloading the page.
